display_note: This value is an adult-skills equivalent from OECD Survey of Adult Skills 2023, derived as 100 minus the share of adults at Level 1 or below in literacy. It is useful for filling high-income literacy gaps, but it is not a direct UNESCO literacy-rate observation and should not be compared one-for-one with administrative literacy series.

This value is a weak-confidence legacy literacy estimate republished from the CIA World Factbook for Greenland rather than a current UIS/World Bank observation. Keep it visually distinct from adult-skills equivalents and from current administrative literacy-rate series.

This value is a weak-confidence legacy literacy estimate republished from the CIA World Factbook for Iceland rather than a current UIS/World Bank observation. Keep it visually distinct from adult-skills equivalents and from current administrative literacy-rate series.

This value comes from an official literacy-rate series and appears to rely on national reporting rather than independent skills assessment. Independent verification is limited, and the latest staged observation is old (2008). Treat this as a weak-confidence published figure, not a directly verified adult-skills estimate.

This near-universal literacy figure is plausible, but it is an administrative/statistical literacy-rate observation, not a direct adult-skills proficiency result. High literacy-rate values can coexist with weaker performance on richer literacy assessments, so avoid reading it as proof of uniformly strong adult reading proficiency.
